pressSubTitle: Chief Minister Shri Bhupendra Patel and Deputy Chief Minister Shri Harsh Sanghavi announce the 'Viksit Gujarat Data Center Policy 2026-29' with the resolve to make Gujarat the country's leading data center hub
pressDesc:
Gandhinagar, 9 July 2026 While launching the 'Viksit Gujarat Data Center Policy 2026-29', Chief Minister Shri Bhupendra said that this policy will make Gujarat the best destination in the country for setting up data centers.

 

The Chief Minister said that under the visionary leadership of Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi, India is emerging as one of the world's largest users and producers of data. In the digital era, data has become a vital asset and is emerging as a new foundation for the economic growth of Viksit Bharat while driving the digital economy forward.

 

 

The launch ceremony of the Viksit Gujarat Data Center Policy 2026-29, prepared by the State Government's Department of Science and Technology, was held at Mahatma Mandir in Gandhinagar in the presence of Deputy Chief Minister Shri Harsh Sanghavi, Science and Technology Minister Shri Arjun Modhwadia, Chief Secretary Shri M. K. Das, and leading representatives from industry and trade.

 

The Chief Minister stated at the event that emerging sectors such as the digital economy, artificial intelligence, cloud computing, and electronics manufacturing have gained fresh momentum, creating immense opportunities for the development of world class digital infrastructure.

 

Under the leadership of Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi, this year's Union Budget has made significant provisions to establish India as a global hub for cloud and AI infrastructure. CM highlighted that the 20 year tax holiday provided to global data center infrastructure providers will serve as a major catalyst for the growth of this sector. He said that under the leadership of the Prime Minister, Gujarat is emerging as a key player in India's digital ecosystem. With the rapid advancement of AI based technologies, the demand for scalable data storage infrastructure is continuously increasing. Keeping this growing demand in mind and with the objective of further strengthening the State's digital economy, the Government has implemented the 'Viksit Gujarat Data Center Policy 2026-29'. He emphasised that the new policy will strengthen and accelerate services such as cloud services, artificial intelligence, digital transactions, e commerce platforms, smart manufacturing systems, and digital governance across the State.

 

The Chief Minister said that the State Government's commitment to long term and sustainable development is clearly reflected in this policy. He added that the Viksit Gujarat Data Center Policy will strengthen Gujarat's digital infrastructure and support the development of next generation digital infrastructure across the country. He remarked that new technologies and innovations will become the backbone of the State's economy in the years ahead.

 

 

Deputy Chief Minister Shri Harsh Sanghavi

 

DyCM said that under the visionary leadership of Prime Minister, Gujarat has become a global brand of trust. Referring to the challenges India faced in the semiconductor and data sectors in the past, he said that although several announcements were made between 1976 and 2009, the projects could not take shape. Under the guidance of the Prime Minister, the Semiconductor Mission was launched in 2021. Within just four months, Gujarat showcased its capabilities by inaugurating more than three Semiconductor OSAT plants.

 

Speaking about Dholera's potential, he said that Dholera alone has received demand exceeding twice the capacity envisaged under the policy, paving the way for it to become the world's largest "Data Center City." Along with establishing data centers, the Government is also actively working towards developing Dholera as the headquarters for Global Capability Centres (GCCs). To strengthen connectivity, a new airport in Dholera will become operational shortly, and a semi high speed train between Ahmedabad and Dholera is also set to commence operations.

 

The Gujarat Government's credibility is so strong that although the policy has been launched today, the Government has already received request letters from leading companies across India and the world seeking capacities nearly double the policy's planned capacity of 7.5 gigawatts.

 

Data centers require significant quantities of electricity and water. Under this policy, 51 percent of the total energy consumed by data centers must mandatorily come from green energy sources. Furthermore, instead of diverting water meant for farmers or industries, water supply will be ensured through desalination plants that convert seawater into usable water.

 

To avoid delays in Government procedures, stakeholder consultation meetings will be organised in the coming days, during which investors will be shown available land pockets. Senior nodal officers will also be appointed to provide handholding support and simplify day to day processes for companies interested in investing in Dholera. Concluding his address, he invited global hyperscale and AI investors to invest in Gujarat and expressed confidence that Gujarat will emerge as the national leader in the data center sector.

 

 

Science and Technology Minister Shri Arjun Modhwadia

 

He said that this policy will establish Gujarat as a global leader in AI and data centers. Under the leadership of Prime Minister and Chief Minister Shri Bhupendra Patel, Gujarat has received proposals for data centers with a total capacity of 10 gigawatts. Of this, the State Government plans to develop up to 8 gigawatts of capacity. This initiative is not limited to data storage alone but is part of creating a holistic AI ecosystem for India and the world.

 

Providing further details, the Minister said that this is the era of artificial intelligence and quantum computing, which are the key pillars of the emerging economy. Gujarat has taken the historic decision to become a prime mover in this new revolution. Compared to the global data center landscape, India still has a long way to go. Today, the United States has around 5,500 data centers and the United Kingdom has more than 500, whereas India has only about 200 data centers. Similarly, India's current data center capacity is only 2 to 3 GW compared to 30 GW in the America, and even after considering upcoming projects, it is unlikely to cross 5 GW.

 

He said that under the leadership of the Chief Minister and the Deputy Chief Minister, the administrative machinery demonstrated extraordinary determination in shaping this historic policy. As a result, Gujarat is poised to become the hub of the AI ecosystem over the next 10 years. He appealed to investors to make substantial investments in this sector.

 

Chief Secretary Shri Manoj Kumar Das described this policy as a game changer in the field of digital infrastructure. He said that the policy has been formulated keeping in view the growing importance of robust digital infrastructure, particularly data centers, in the era of artificial intelligence and machine learning. He highlighted that Gujarat is well equipped to meet the substantial water and electricity requirements of data centers due to its extensive coastline and abundant renewable energy resources such as solar and wind power. Through this new policy, Gujarat is expected to attract investments of approximately Rs. 6 lakh crore and generate large scale employment opportunities, which, according to the World Bank's classification, will further strengthen Gujarat's position as a high middle income economy.

 

Secretary of the Department of Science and Technology, Dr. P. Bharati, presented the key features of the policy.

 

This event was attended by ​Additional Chief Secretary of the Finance Department Shri T. Natarajan, Additional Chief Secretary of the Industries and Mines Department Smt. Mamta Verma, industry leaders, investors, and subject matter experts.

 

-------
